def_en: A very large quantity of something; plenty
quote_en: The garden produced an abundance of tomatoes and cucumbers this summer.
quote_zh: 今年夏天花园出产了丰富的西红柿和黄瓜。
ex_en: True abundance is not having more than you need, but needing less than you have.
ex_zh: 真正的富足不是拥有超过所需，而是所需少于拥有。

def_en: To gather or build up over time
quote_en: Dust will accumulate on shelves if you don't clean them regularly.
quote_zh: 如果不定期打扫，架子上会积灰尘。
ex_en: Small acts of kindness, like drops of rain, accumulate into rivers that can change the world.
ex_zh: 小小的善举如同雨滴，汇聚成能够改变世界的河流。

def_en: Knowing someone slightly; familiar with something
quote_en: I became acquainted with my neighbor while walking our dogs in the park.
quote_zh: 我在公园遛狗时认识了邻居。
ex_en: To be acquainted with suffering is not a curse; it is the forge where compassion is hammered into shape.
ex_zh: 认识苦难并非诅咒，那是将同情锤炼成形的熔炉。

def_en: Sharp or severe in effect; very serious; keenly perceptive
quote_en: The patient felt an acute pain in his lower back after lifting the heavy box.
quote_zh: 病人抬起重箱后感到腰部剧痛。
ex_en: An acute mind can solve puzzles, but an acute heart sees the hidden tears behind a smile.
ex_zh: 敏锐的头脑能解谜题，敏锐的心却能看见笑容背后隐藏的泪水。

def_en: A substance added to something in small amounts to improve or preserve it
quote_en: Many breakfast cereals contain added vitamins and mineral additives.
quote_zh: 许多早餐麦片含有添加的维生素和矿物质添加剂。
ex_en: Be careful what additives you mix into your character—honesty is the only preservative that never expires.
ex_zh: 小心你往品格里掺入什么添加剂——诚实是唯一永不失效的防腐剂。

def_en: Enough or good enough for what is required
quote_en: A sandwich and an apple provide adequate nutrition for a school lunch.
quote_zh: 一个三明治和一个苹果为学校午餐提供足够的营养。
ex_en: Having adequate resources is useful, but having adequate gratitude turns every meal into a feast.
ex_zh: 拥有足够的资源是有用的，但拥有足够的感恩能让每一餐变成盛宴。

def_en: A person or country that attacks first or starts a conflict
quote_en: The United Nations condemned the aggressor for invading its neighbor's territory.
quote_zh: 联合国谴责侵略者入侵邻国领土。
ex_en: The first aggressor in any argument is usually fear, not anger—defeat the fear, and you disarm the fight.
ex_zh: 任何争论中的第一个挑衅者通常是恐惧而非愤怒——打败恐惧，你就解除了战斗的武装。

def_en: Able to move quickly and easily; mentally quick
quote_en: The agile monkey swung from branch to branch without ever falling.
quote_zh: 敏捷的猴子在树枝间荡来荡去，从不掉落。
ex_en: An agile body escapes obstacles; an agile mind turns obstacles into stepping stones.
ex_zh: 敏捷的身体能避开障碍；敏捷的头脑却能把障碍变成垫脚石。

def_en: The repetition of the same sound or letter at the beginning of nearby words
quote_en: "Peter picked pickled peppers" is a famous example of alliteration.
quote_zh: “彼得摘了腌辣椒”是头韵的著名例子。
ex_en: Alliteration pleases the ear, but truth pleases the heart—let your words dance, but let them also mean something.
ex_zh: 头韵悦耳，真理悦心——让你的词语舞动，但也让它们有意义。

def_en: A high-ranking diplomat who represents their country in another country
quote_en: The ambassador hosted a dinner to improve relations between the two nations.
quote_zh: 大使举办晚宴以改善两国关系。
ex_en: Be an ambassador of kindness wherever you go; you never know which small gesture might end a silent war.
ex_zh: 无论走到哪里，都做善意的使者；你永远不知道哪个小小的举动可能结束一场无声的战争。

def_en: Plenty of; more than enough
quote_en: There is ample space in the garage for two cars and a bicycle.
quote_zh: 车库里有充足的空间停放两辆车和一辆自行车。
ex_en: Ample evidence can prove a fact, but only ample love can prove a person's worth.
ex_zh: 充足的证据能证明事实，但只有充足的爱能证明一个人的价值。

def_en: Not named or identified; done by someone unknown
quote_en: The donation was made by an anonymous donor who wished to remain private.
quote_zh: 捐款来自一位希望保持隐私的匿名捐赠者。
ex_en: To do good and remain anonymous is to plant a tree whose shade you will never sit in—that is pure generosity.
ex_zh: 行善不留名，等于种下一棵你永远不会坐在其树荫下的树——那才是纯粹的慷慨。

def_en: A valuable old object; belonging to an earlier period
quote_en: She inherited an antique clock that has been in her family for over a hundred years.
quote_zh: 她继承了一座在她家传承了上百年的古董钟。
ex_en: Don't treat old friends like antiques—admired but untouched. Polish them with your presence.
ex_zh: 不要把老朋友当成古董——只欣赏却不触碰。用你的陪伴去擦拭他们。

def_en: A section of extra information at the end of a book; a small organ in the body
quote_en: The appendix at the back of the textbook contains maps and timelines.
quote_zh: 教科书末尾的附录包含地图和时间线。
ex_en: The appendix of your life is not the years at the end, but the memories you forgot to write into the main story.
ex_zh: 人生的附录不是最后的岁月，而是你忘记写入主线故事的那些记忆。

def_en: A formal request for something; the act of putting something into practice
quote_en: His application for the summer internship included three letters of recommendation.
quote_zh: 他的暑期实习申请附有三封推荐信。
ex_en: Knowledge without application is like a key that never turns a lock—useless, no matter how beautifully carved.
ex_zh: 没有应用的知识，就像一把从未转动过锁的钥匙——无论雕琢得多美，都毫无用处。

def_en: Almost correct or exact; to come close to something
quote_en: The approximate number of students in the school is 500, give or take a few.
quote_zh: 学校学生的大约人数是500，上下浮动几个。
ex_en: Do not approximate the truth to make it comfortable; a rounded corner is still a corner, not a circle.
ex_zh: 不要为了让真相舒服而把它近似化；一个磨圆的角依然是角，不是圆。

def_en: The study of ancient peoples through their artifacts and ruins
quote_en: Archaeology helps us understand how people lived thousands of years ago.
quote_zh: 考古学帮助我们了解数千年前人们的生活方式。
ex_en: We practice archaeology of the soul when we dig through past mistakes to find the treasure of wisdom buried beneath shame.
ex_zh: 当我们挖掘过去的错误，寻找掩埋在羞愧之下的智慧宝藏时，我们就在进行灵魂的考古学。

def_en: A member of the highest social class, often with inherited titles
quote_en: The aristocrat lived in a grand castle passed down through generations.
quote_zh: 那位贵族住在一座代代相传的宏伟城堡里。
ex_en: Birth may make you an aristocrat, but only character can make you a noble soul.
ex_zh: 出身可以让你成为贵族，但只有品格能让你成为高贵的灵魂。

def_en: A particular part or feature of something; a way of looking at something
quote_en: One important aspect of learning a language is practicing speaking every day.
quote_zh: 学习语言的一个重要方面是每天练习口语。
ex_en: Every problem has an aspect that gratitude can illuminate—seek it, and the darkness shrinks.
ex_zh: 每个问题都有一个感恩能照亮的方面——找到它，黑暗就会缩小。

def_en: To state firmly or confidently; to defend one's rights
quote_en: The witness was brave enough to assert the truth in front of the whole court.
quote_zh: 证人足够勇敢，在整座法庭面前断言了真相。
ex_en: To assert your opinion is human; to listen after asserting is wisdom.
ex_zh: 坚持自己的观点是人之常情；坚持之后倾听则是智慧。

def_en: A mixed collection or variety of things
quote_en: The bakery sold an assortment of cookies, including chocolate chip and oatmeal raisin.
quote_zh: 面包店出售各种各样的饼干，包括巧克力碎和燕麦葡萄干。
ex_en: Life gives you an assortment of joys and sorrows; wisdom is knowing how to savor one without fearing the other.
ex_zh: 生活给你各种各样的快乐与悲伤；智慧在于懂得如何品味一种而不惧怕另一种。

def_en: To succeed in reaching or achieving something through effort
quote_en: After months of practice, she finally attained her goal of swimming a mile.
quote_zh: 经过几个月的练习，她终于达到了游一英里的目标。
ex_en: You may attain wealth by climbing, but you attain peace only by learning when to stop climbing.
ex_zh: 攀登可以让你获得财富，但只有学会何时停止攀登，你才能获得平静。

def_en: Loud enough to be heard
quote_en: The teacher’s whisper was barely audible at the back of the classroom.
quote_zh: 老师的耳语在教室后排几乎听不见。
ex_en: The most audible voice in a room is not always the truest; listen for the quiet words that tremble with honesty.
ex_zh: 房间里最响亮的声音不一定最真实；去寻找那些因诚实而颤抖的安静话语。

def_en: A short performance to test an actor's or musician's ability; to perform such a test
quote_en: She practiced her monologue for weeks before her audition for the school play.
quote_zh: 她在参加学校话剧试演前，花了数周练习独白。
ex_en: Every day is an audition for the role of your future self—act with kindness, and you will be cast.
ex_zh: 每一天都是为未来自己这个角色的试演——用善意去表演，你就会被选中。

def_en: A large room or building where an audience sits to watch a performance
quote_en: The entire school gathered in the auditorium for the annual talent show.
quote_zh: 全校师生聚集在礼堂观看一年一度的才艺秀。
ex_en: In the auditorium of life, you are both the performer and the audience; do not forget to applaud others.
ex_zh: 在人生的礼堂里，你既是表演者又是观众；不要忘记为他人鼓掌。

def_en: Relating to the sense of hearing
quote_en: Owls have excellent auditory abilities that help them hunt at night.
quote_zh: 猫头鹰拥有极佳的听觉能力，帮助它们在夜间捕猎。
ex_en: Your auditory nerves may fade with age, but the ability to truly listen—to a friend’s pain—can grow forever.
ex_zh: 听觉神经会随年龄衰退，但真正倾听——朋友痛苦的能力——却能永远增长。

def_en: A large mass of snow, ice, and rocks that slides rapidly down a mountainside
quote_en: Skiers must check the weather report to avoid triggering an avalanche.
quote_zh: 滑雪者必须查看天气预报，以免引发雪崩。
ex_en: A single unkind word can start an avalanche of regret; speak gently, even when you are right.
ex_zh: 一句不友善的话可能引发悔恨的雪崩；即使你正确，也要温柔地说话。

def_en: A slow, sentimental song or poem that tells a story
quote_en: The old sailor sang a haunting ballad about a shipwreck and lost love.
quote_zh: 老水手唱了一首关于海难和失去爱情、令人难忘的民谣。
ex_en: Your life is a ballad no one else can write; let each verse be true, even if it is not perfect.
ex_zh: 你的人生是一首别人无法写就的民谣；让每一节都真实，即使它不完美。

def_en: Heavy material placed in a ship's hold to keep it stable
quote_en: The ship took on extra ballast to stay steady during the storm.
quote_zh: 船装载了额外的压舱物以便在风暴中保持平稳。
ex_en: Humility is the ballast of the soul; without it, the slightest praise can tip you over.
ex_zh: 谦逊是灵魂的压舱物；没有它，最微小的赞美也能将你掀翻。

def_en: To officially forbid something; an official prohibition
quote_en: The city voted to ban single-use plastic bags to protect the environment.
quote_zh: 城市投票禁止一次性塑料袋以保护环境。
ex_en: To ban a book is to fear an idea; to read it and still disagree—that is courage.
ex_zh: 禁止一本书是害怕一个想法；读了它仍然不同意——那才是勇气。

def_en: Unable to pay debts; to cause to become financially ruined
quote_en: The company went bankrupt after years of poor management.
quote_zh: 公司因多年管理不善而破产。
ex_en: A person can be bankrupt in money but rich in spirit; never trade your integrity for a full wallet.
ex_zh: 一个人可能在金钱上破产，但精神上富有；永远不要为了装满的钱包而出卖正直。

def_en: A makeshift barrier to block passage; to block with such a barrier
quote_en: Protesters built a barricade of overturned cars and trash bins.
quote_zh: 抗议者用翻倒的汽车和垃圾桶建起了路障。
ex_en: The strongest barricade against regret is a moment of pause before anger speaks.
ex_zh: 抵御后悔的最强路障，是在愤怒开口前暂停的那一瞬。

def_en: Stuck unable to move because there is no wind (of a ship)
quote_en: The sailboat lay becalmed in the middle of the lake for three hours.
quote_zh: 帆船在湖中央因无风而停航了三个小时。
ex_en: When your life feels becalmed, do not curse the stillness—use it to repair your sails.
ex_zh: 当你感觉人生停滞不前时，不要咒骂那份平静——用它来修补你的船帆。

def_en: To become friends with someone, especially when they need help
quote_en: The new student was shy, so Maria decided to befriend her at lunch.
quote_zh: 新来的学生很害羞，所以玛丽亚决定在午餐时与她交朋友。
ex_en: To befriend a stranger is to plant a flag of peace in unmapped territory.
ex_zh: 与陌生人交朋友，是在未知的土地上插上一面和平的旗帜。

def_en: To confuse or perplex someone completely
quote_en: The complicated math problem completely befuddled the entire class.
quote_zh: 这道复杂的数学题让全班同学都完全糊涂了。
ex_en: Do not let fancy words befuddle you; truth speaks clearly, even when it wears simple clothes.
ex_zh: 别让华丽的词藻迷惑你；真理说话清晰，即使它穿着朴素的衣裳。

def_en: To express sadness or disappointment about something
quote_en: The farmer bemoaned the lack of rain after three weeks of drought.
quote_zh: 干旱三周后，农夫哀叹缺乏雨水。
ex_en: It is easy to bemoan what you lack; it takes courage to celebrate what you have.
ex_zh: 哀叹自己所缺很容易；庆祝自己拥有则需要勇气。

def_en: Swollen or puffed up, often from excess gas or overeating
quote_en: After drinking too much soda, his stomach felt bloated and uncomfortable.
quote_zh: 喝完太多汽水后，他的胃感觉又胀又不舒服。
ex_en: A bloated ego is like a balloon—one sharp word from the truth, and it bursts into nothing.
ex_zh: 膨胀的自我就像气球——真相的一句锋利话语，就能让它炸得无影无踪。

def_en: A small, thin book with paper covers
quote_en: The museum gave us a booklet explaining the history of each painting.
quote_zh: 博物馆给了我们一本小册子，解释了每幅画的历史。
ex_en: Do not judge a person by the booklet of their resume; read the novel of their actions instead.
ex_zh: 不要用简历那本小册子来判断一个人；去读他们行动的长篇小说。

def_en: Generously given or giving; abundant
quote_en: The autumn harvest brought a bounteous supply of apples and pumpkins.
quote_zh: 秋收带来了丰盛的苹果和南瓜。
ex_en: A bounteous heart does not count the cost of kindness; it gives like the sun gives light—freely.
ex_zh: 慷慨的心不计较善意的代价；它像太阳给予光一样给予——无偿地。

def_en: To bend forward at the waist as a sign of respect; the front of a ship
quote_en: In Japan, it is polite to bow when greeting someone older or of higher status.
quote_zh: 在日本，问候年长者或地位更高者时鞠躬是礼貌的。
ex_en: You can bow to authority without breaking your spine, as long as you never bow to injustice.
ex_zh: 你可以向权威鞠躬而不折断脊梁，只要你从不向不公正低头。

def_en: A device for slowing a vehicle; to slow or stop using a brake
quote_en: The cyclist squeezed both brakes to stop before the intersection.
quote_zh: 骑车人捏紧两个刹车，在十字路口前停了下来。
ex_en: Sometimes the kindest thing you can do is apply the brake—to a harsh word, to a hasty promise.
ex_zh: 有时你能做的最善良的事，就是踩下刹车——对一句伤人的话，对一个草率的承诺。

def_en: To separate into pieces; to interrupt; a pause or fracture
quote_en: Be careful not to break the vase; it was your grandmother’s favorite.
quote_zh: 小心别打碎花瓶；那是你祖母最喜欢的。
ex_en: It is better to break a bad habit than to be broken by it.
ex_zh: 打破一个坏习惯，胜过被它打垮。

def_en: A rich fabric woven with raised patterns of gold or silver thread
quote_en: The queen’s gown was made of gold brocade and took months to sew.
quote_zh: 女王的礼服由金色锦缎制成，缝制了数月。
ex_en: Outer brocade may impress a crowd, but inner kindness impresses the soul.
ex_zh: 外表的锦缎能打动人群，但内心的善良能打动灵魂。

def_en: A wild or untamed horse of western North America
quote_en: The cowboy tried to ride the bronco, but it bucked him off in seconds.
quote_zh: 牛仔试图骑那匹野马，但几秒钟内就被甩了下来。
ex_en: A bronco spirit is not meant for a stable; some souls need the freedom of open plains.
ex_zh: 野马的精神不适合马厩；有些灵魂需要开阔平原的自由。

def_en: To move busily and energetically; noisy activity
quote_en: The train station bustled with travelers rushing to catch their trains.
quote_zh: 火车站里满是匆忙赶火车的旅客，一片忙碌。
ex_en: In the bustle of life, do not forget the stillness where your best ideas are born.
ex_zh: 在生活的忙碌中，不要忘记那个让你最好想法诞生的安静。

def_en: A small shelter or cabin by a pool or beach
quote_en: They changed into their swimsuits inside the cabana before running to the water.
quote_zh: 他们在泳池小屋换好泳衣，然后跑向水边。
ex_en: A cabana offers shade, but your own heart offers the truest shelter from life’s heat.
ex_zh: 泳池小屋提供阴凉，但你自己内心提供抵御生活之热的最真实庇护所。

def_en: A chemical element important for strong bones and teeth
quote_en: Drinking milk helps children get enough calcium for growing bones.
quote_zh: 喝牛奶帮助儿童获得足够的钙以促进骨骼生长。
ex_en: Build a skeleton of calcium, but build a character of courage—that frame will never break.
ex_zh: 用钙建造骨骼，但用勇气建造品格——那个框架永远不会断裂。

def_en: A unit of energy from food; the energy needed to raise water temperature
quote_en: An apple contains about 95 calories, while a slice of cake has over 300.
quote_zh: 一个苹果含有大约95卡路里，而一块蛋糕有超过300卡路里。
ex_en: Count calories for your body, but count kindnesses for your soul—both need the right fuel.
ex_zh: 为身体计算卡路里，但为灵魂计算善举——两者都需要正确的燃料。

def_en: A man-made waterway built for boats or irrigation
quote_en: The Panama Canal allows ships to travel between the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans.
quote_zh: 巴拿马运河允许船只在大西洋和太平洋之间航行。
ex_en: Dig a canal of patience through the mountain of anger, and let understanding flow.
ex_zh: 在愤怒的大山中挖掘一条耐心的运河，让理解流淌。

def_en: The maximum amount something can hold; the ability to do something
quote_en: The stadium has a capacity of 50,000 people for football games.
quote_zh: 体育场举办足球比赛时可容纳五万人。
ex_en: Your capacity to love is not fixed like a cup; it expands every time you choose kindness over fear.
ex_zh: 你爱人的能力不像杯子那样固定；每当你选择善意而非恐惧时，它就会扩大。

def_en: A loose garment worn over the shoulders; a point of land extending into the sea
quote_en: The superhero wore a red cape that fluttered behind him as he flew.
quote_zh: 超级英雄披着红色披肩，飞行时在身后飘动。
ex_en: A cape of courage is not worn on the outside; it is woven from the threads of every small brave choice.
ex_zh: 勇气的披肩不是穿在外面的，它由每一个小小的勇敢选择的丝线织成。

def_en: To overturn in the water
quote_en: A large wave caused the small rowboat to capsize near the shore.
quote_zh: 一个大浪使小船在岸边附近倾覆。
ex_en: Do not let one failure capsize your entire journey; right your boat and sail again.
ex_zh: 别让一次失败颠覆你的整个旅程；扶正你的船，再次起航。

def_en: Goods carried by a ship, plane, or vehicle
quote_en: The ship’s cargo included coffee beans, bananas, and cotton.
quote_zh: 船的货物包括咖啡豆、香蕉和棉花。
ex_en: The heaviest cargo any person carries is not in the hold—it is the weight of unspoken regret.
ex_zh: 任何人携带的最重货物不在船舱里——那是未说出口的悔恨的重量。

def_en: A dish of meat and vegetables cooked slowly in an oven
quote_en: Mom made a chicken casserole with rice, carrots, and peas for dinner.
quote_zh: 妈妈用米饭、胡萝卜和豌豆做了一道鸡肉砂锅菜当晚餐。
ex_en: A family is like a casserole: different ingredients, but together they create something warm and nourishing.
ex_zh: 家庭就像砂锅菜：不同的食材，但在一起创造出了温暖而滋养的东西。

def_en: A device that throws objects through the air; to launch suddenly
quote_en: Medieval armies used a catapult to hurl giant stones at castle walls.
quote_zh: 中世纪的军队用弹射器向城墙投掷巨石。
ex_en: Kindness can catapult a stranger from despair to hope faster than any machine.
ex_zh: 善意能比任何机器更快地将陌生人从绝望弹射到希望。

def_en: Relating to the sky or outer space; heavenly
quote_en: The telescope allowed us to see celestial bodies like Jupiter and its moons.
quote_zh: 望远镜让我们看到了木星及其卫星等天体。
ex_en: Keep your eyes on celestial goals, but plant your feet firmly on earthly kindness.
ex_zh: 目光瞄准天上的目标，但双脚稳稳踏在人间的善意上。

def_en: A fatty substance in the blood that can block arteries if too high
quote_en: Eating too much fried food can raise your cholesterol levels.
quote_zh: 吃太多油炸食品会升高你的胆固醇水平。
ex_en: Clear the cholesterol of envy from your heart, and let the blood of generosity flow freely.
ex_zh: 清除心中嫉妒的胆固醇，让慷慨的血液自由流淌。

def_en: To move continuously through a closed system; to spread
quote_en: The fan helps circulate cool air throughout the room.
quote_zh: 风扇帮助冷空气在整个房间循环。
ex_en: Kind words circulate like fresh air; once released, they return to refresh the speaker.
ex_zh: 善意的话语像新鲜空气一样循环；一旦说出，它们会回来滋润说话者。

def_en: The distance around a circle or round object
quote_en: To find the circumference of a circle, multiply its diameter by pi.
quote_zh: 要计算圆的周长，用直径乘以圆周率。
ex_en: The circumference of your worries may seem endless, but every circle has a center—return to yours.
ex_zh: 你担忧的周长看似无尽，但每个圆都有一个圆心——回到你的圆心去。

def_en: An advanced stage of human social development
quote_en: Ancient Egyptian civilization built pyramids that still stand today.
quote_zh: 古埃及文明建造的金字塔至今依然矗立。
ex_en: A civilization is measured not by its tallest towers, but by how it treats its lowest citizens.
ex_zh: 一个文明的衡量标准不是它最高的塔楼，而是它如何对待最底层的公民。

def_en: A group of families with a common ancestor; a close-knit group
quote_en: The Scottish clan gathered every summer for games and celebrations.
quote_zh: 苏格兰氏族每年夏天聚集举行运动会和庆典。
ex_en: Your true clan is not defined by blood, but by those who stand with you in silence when you need them most.
ex_zh: 你真正的氏族不是由血缘定义的，而是由那些在你最需要时默默站在你身边的人。

def_en: The usual weather conditions in a region
quote_en: The Mediterranean climate has mild winters and hot, dry summers.
quote_zh: 地中海气候冬季温和，夏季炎热干燥。
ex_en: The climate of your mind determines the harvest of your life—plant optimism.
ex_zh: 你心灵的气候决定你人生的收成——种下乐观。

def_en: Rough in texture; crude or vulgar in manner
quote_en: The burlap sack had a coarse surface that irritated her skin.
quote_zh: 粗麻布袋表面粗糙，刺激了她的皮肤。
ex_en: A coarse tongue may win a laugh, but a gentle tongue wins a friend for life.
ex_zh: 粗俗的舌头能换来一笑，但温柔的舌头能赢得一生的朋友。

def_en: An artwork made by gluing different materials onto a surface
quote_en: For art class, she made a collage using magazine cutouts and fabric scraps.
quote_zh: 美术课上，她用杂志剪图和碎布做了一幅拼贴画。
ex_en: Your memories are a collage—don't let a few dark pieces hide the bright ones.
ex_zh: 你的记忆是一幅拼贴画——别让几块暗色的碎片遮住那些明亮的。

def_en: A promise or dedication to a cause or person
quote_en: Her commitment to practicing the violin every day helped her win first prize.
quote_zh: 她每天练习小提琴的奉献精神帮助她赢得了一等奖。
ex_en: Commitment is not a chain; it is the anchor that lets you sail without drifting.
ex_zh: 承诺不是锁链；它是让你航行而不漂流的锚。

def_en: Noisy confusion or disturbance
quote_en: The loud crash caused a commotion in the otherwise quiet library.
quote_zh: 巨大的撞击声在原本安静的图书馆里引起了一阵骚动。
ex_en: Do not mistake commotion for progress; a boiling pot makes noise, but a growing tree is silent.
ex_zh: 别把喧闹误认为进步；沸腾的锅发出噪音，而生长的树却是沉默的。

def_en: To understand something fully
quote_en: It took him a while to comprehend the instructions for building the model airplane.
quote_zh: 他花了一些时间才理解组装模型飞机的说明。
ex_en: You can comprehend the stars with your mind, but only your heart can comprehend why one star matters.
ex_zh: 你可以用头脑理解星星，但只有你的心能理解为什么某一颗星星很重要。

def_en: To prepare by mixing ingredients; to invent a story or plan
quote_en: The chef concocted a delicious new sauce from tomatoes and herbs.
quote_zh: 厨师用西红柿和香草调制出了一种美味的新酱汁。
ex_en: Do not concoct fears about tomorrow; most monsters you imagine never show up.
ex_zh: 不要编造对明天的恐惧；你想象的大多数怪兽根本不会出现。

def_en: Identical in shape and size; in agreement
quote_en: In geometry class, we learned that two triangles are congruent if all their sides match.
quote_zh: 几何课上，我们学到如果两个三角形的所有边都相等，它们就是全等的。
ex_en: Let your actions be congruent with your words; a straight line needs no apology.
ex_zh: 让你的行动与言语一致；一条直线不需要道歉。

def_en: Large or great in size, amount, or importance
quote_en: The storm caused considerable damage to the coastal town.
quote_zh: 风暴对沿海小镇造成了相当大的破坏。
ex_en: A considerable fortune can be lost overnight, but a considerable character endures every storm.
ex_zh: 可观的财富可能一夜消失，但可观的品格能经受每一场风暴。

def_en: Behaving the same way over time; unchanging
quote_en: The team’s consistent practice led to a winning season.
quote_zh: 团队持续的训练带来了一个胜利的赛季。
ex_en: Be consistent in small kindnesses; the river carves canyons not by force, but by never stopping.
ex_zh: 在小善举上保持一致；河流雕刻峡谷靠的不是力量，而是永不停止。

def_en: To eat, drink, or use up; to destroy completely
quote_en: A large forest fire can consume thousands of acres in a single day.
quote_zh: 一场大森林火灾能在一天内吞噬数千英亩。
ex_en: Do not let envy consume you; it is a fire that burns only its carrier.
ex_zh: 别让嫉妒吞噬你；那是一场只灼烧携带者的火。

def_en: The act of using up resources; the amount consumed
quote_en: Reducing energy consumption helps protect the environment.
quote_zh: 减少能源消耗有助于保护环境。
ex_en: Mindless consumption fills your house; mindful living fills your soul.
ex_zh: 无意识的消费填满你的房子；有意识的生活填满你的灵魂。

def_en: Heated disagreement; a claim or argument
quote_en: The main point of contention between the siblings was who would use the computer first.
quote_zh: 兄妹俩争论的焦点是谁先用电脑。
ex_en: Avoid contention where no truth is at stake; some battles are won only by not fighting.
ex_zh: 在不涉及真理的地方避免争论；有些战斗只有通过不战斗才能赢得。

def_en: One of the seven large landmasses on Earth
quote_en: Africa is the second largest continent, after Asia.
quote_zh: 非洲是仅次于亚洲的第二大洲。
ex_en: Travel across a continent and you may find yourself; travel inside your heart and you may find everyone.
ex_zh: 穿越一个大陆，你可能会找到自己；走进你的内心，你可能会找到所有人。

def_en: Opposite in nature or direction; an opposite fact
quote_en: Contrary to what his friends thought, he actually enjoyed reading.
quote_zh: 与他朋友们想的相反，他其实很喜欢阅读。
ex_en: The contrary wind teaches the sailor more than the calm sea ever could.
ex_zh: 逆风比平静的大海更能教会水手。

def_en: To persuade someone to believe something
quote_en: She used clear evidence to convince the jury that the defendant was innocent.
quote_zh: 她用清晰的证据说服陪审团被告是无辜的。
ex_en: You cannot convince a closed mind, but you can open a window—and sometimes that is enough.
ex_zh: 你无法说服封闭的头脑，但你可以打开一扇窗——有时这就足够了。

def_en: A pen for horses or cattle; to gather into a corral
quote_en: The cowboys herded the cattle into the wooden corral for the night.
quote_zh: 牛仔们把牛群赶进木制畜栏过夜。
ex_en: Do not corral your dreams into what others say is possible; let them run wild.
ex_zh: 不要把你的梦想圈进别人所说的“可能”的畜栏里；让它们自由奔跑。

def_en: To match or be similar; to exchange letters
quote_en: The symbols on the map correspond to real landmarks in the park.
quote_zh: 地图上的符号与公园里的真实地标相对应。
ex_en: Let your actions correspond to your beliefs; a life of integrity writes no conflicting letters.
ex_zh: 让你的行动与信念相符；正直的人生不会写出矛盾的信件。

def_en: Dishonest for personal gain; to make someone dishonest
quote_en: The corrupt official accepted bribes to ignore building safety violations.
quote_zh: 腐败的官员收受贿赂，对建筑安全违规视而不见。
ex_en: Power does not corrupt everyone; it only reveals what was already hidden in the heart.
ex_zh: 权力不会腐蚀所有人；它只是揭示了早已藏在心底的东西。

def_en: A group of people chosen to make decisions or give advice
quote_en: The student council voted to organize a charity bake sale.
quote_zh: 学生会投票决定组织一场慈善烘焙义卖。
ex_en: A wise council listens more than it speaks; a foolish council is just noise in a circle.
ex_zh: 明智的委员会倾听多于发言；愚蠢的委员会只是围成一圈的噪音。

def_en: Advice or guidance; to give advice
quote_en: The school counselor offered counsel to students struggling with homework.
quote_zh: 学校辅导员给作业有困难的学生提供建议。
ex_en: The best counsel you will ever receive is the voice you hear when everyone else has stopped talking.
ex_zh: 你收到的最好建议，是当所有人都停止说话时你听到的那个声音。

def_en: Two lines of poetry that rhyme and have the same meter
quote_en: The poem ended with a rhyming couplet: “The sun did set, the day did end / My dearest love, my only friend.”
quote_zh: 这首诗以一个押韵的对句结尾。
ex_en: A couplet can hold a universe in two lines; so can a single breath of forgiveness.
ex_zh: 一个对句能在两行中容纳一个宇宙；一次宽恕的呼吸也能做到。

def_en: The path or direction something takes; a series of lessons
quote_en: The river changed its course after the heavy rains flooded the valley.
quote_zh: 大雨淹没山谷后，河流改变了路线。
ex_en: You cannot change the course of the wind, but you can adjust your sails—that is freedom.
ex_zh: 你无法改变风的路线，但可以调整你的船帆——那就是自由。

def_en: A skilled worker who makes things by hand
quote_en: The carpenter was a true craftsman who built cabinets without using a single nail.
quote_zh: 那位木匠是真正的工匠，不用一颗钉子就造出了柜子。
ex_en: A craftsman loves the process, not just the product; love the becoming, not just the arrived.
ex_zh: 工匠热爱过程，而不仅仅是成品；热爱“成为”，而不仅仅是“成为之后”。

def_en: To want something very strongly
quote_en: After running for an hour, he craved a cold glass of water.
quote_zh: 跑了一小时后，他渴望喝一杯冷水。
ex_en: Do not crave the approval of everyone; a tree does not crave the opinion of the wind.
ex_zh: 不要渴望所有人的认可；一棵树不会渴望风对它评头论足。

def_en: Praise or recognition; belief in someone’s ability; to believe
quote_en: She took full credit for the project, even though her team did most of the work.
quote_zh: 她将项目的全部功劳归于自己，尽管大部分工作是她的团队做的。
ex_en: Give credit where it is due, and you will never lack for collaborators.
ex_zh: 在应得之处给予赞扬，你将永远不会缺少合作者。

def_en: The scientific study of crime and criminals
quote_en: Criminology helps police understand why people commit crimes and how to prevent them.
quote_zh: 犯罪学帮助警方理解人们为何犯罪以及如何预防。
ex_en: Criminology studies the broken law; wisdom studies the broken heart behind it.
ex_zh: 犯罪学研究被违反的法律；智慧研究法律背后的破碎之心。

def_en: The customs, arts, and way of life of a group of people
quote_en: Learning about Japanese culture includes studying tea ceremonies and origami.
quote_zh: 了解日本文化包括学习茶道和折纸。
ex_en: Culture is what a people remembers; character is what a person remembers when no one is watching.
ex_zh: 文化是一个民族记住的东西；品格是当没人在看时一个人记住的东西。

def_en: Awkward to carry or manage because of size or weight
quote_en: The old suitcase was cumbersome and difficult to drag through the airport.
quote_zh: 旧行李箱很笨重，在机场里拖行很困难。
ex_en: A cumbersome ego is heavier than any luggage; drop it, and you will travel faster.
ex_zh: 笨重的自我比任何行李都重；放下它，你会走得更快。

def_en: A time by which someone must be home or off the streets
quote_en: The town imposed a curfew, requiring everyone to be indoors by 10 p.m.
quote_zh: 小镇实行宵禁，要求所有人晚上10点前待在室内。
ex_en: A self-imposed curfew on complaining can be the first step toward a grateful heart.
ex_zh: 对抱怨实施自我宵禁，可能是走向感恩之心的第一步。

def_en: The system of money used in a country
quote_en: The local currency in Japan is the yen, not the dollar.
quote_zh: 日本的当地货币是日元，不是美元。
ex_en: Kindness is a currency every country accepts; invest it everywhere you go.
ex_zh: 善意是每个国家都接受的货币；无论走到哪里，都去投资它。

def_en: Shaped like a cylinder (a tube with circular ends)
quote_en: A soda can is cylindrical, with a flat top and bottom and curved sides.
quote_zh: 汽水罐是圆柱形的，有平坦的顶部和底部以及弯曲的侧面。
ex_en: A cylindrical lens sees only straight lines; open your mind to curves and colors.
ex_zh: 圆柱形的透镜只能看到直线；打开你的思想去迎接曲线和色彩。

def_en: Seeming difficult to deal with; frightening
def_zh: 令人却步的；使人气馁的
quote_en: Climbing the mountain seemed daunting, but they took it one step at a time.
quote_zh: 攀登那座山看似令人生畏，但他们一步一步地完成了。
ex_en: Every daunting journey is just a series of small, brave steps—take the first one now.
ex_zh: 每一次令人生畏的旅程，都只是一系列小小的勇敢步伐——现在就迈出第一步。

def_en: To waste time by moving slowly
quote_en: Stop dawdling and finish your homework, or you will be late for dinner.
quote_zh: 别磨蹭了，快写完作业，否则你赶不上晚饭了。
ex_en: To dawdle is human; to know when to stop dawdling is wisdom.
ex_zh: 磨蹭是人之常情；知道何时停止磨蹭才是智慧。

def_en: Scattered pieces of waste or remains
quote_en: After the storm, the yard was covered with debris from broken tree branches.
quote_zh: 暴风雨后，院子里满是断裂树枝的碎片。
ex_en: Clear the debris of old grudges from your heart, and let new friendships grow.
ex_zh: 清除心中旧怨的碎片，让新的友谊生长。

def_en: To make someone believe something that is not true
quote_en: The dishonest salesman tried to deceive the elderly couple into paying too much.
quote_zh: 不诚实的推销员试图欺骗那对老夫妇支付过高的价格。
ex_en: The easiest person to deceive is yourself; the hardest to face is the truth you hid.
ex_zh: 最容易欺骗的人是自己；最难面对的是你隐藏的真相。

def_en: The act of deceiving; a trick or lie
quote_en: The spy’s deception fooled the enemy for nearly a year.
quote_zh: 间谍的欺骗手段愚弄了敌人近一年。
ex_en: Deception builds a house of cards; one honest breeze, and it all falls down.
ex_zh: 欺骗建造的是纸牌屋；一阵诚实的微风，它就全部倒塌。

def_en: To figure out the meaning of something unclear or coded
quote_en: The codebreaker worked for hours to decipher the enemy’s secret message.
quote_zh: 密码破译员花了数小时破译敌人的秘密信息。
ex_en: Learn to decipher the silence between words; that is where truth often hides.
ex_zh: 学会解读词语之间的沉默；真理常常藏在那里。

def_en: A lack or shortage of something needed
quote_en: A deficiency of vitamin C can lead to a disease called scurvy.
quote_zh: 缺乏维生素C会导致一种叫做坏血病的疾病。
ex_en: A deficiency of sleep can be fixed in a night; a deficiency of kindness takes a lifetime to cure.
ex_zh: 睡眠不足可以一夜弥补；善意的不足需要一生来治愈。

def_en: A sad, depressed state of mind
quote_en: After losing the championship game, the team walked off the field in dejection.
quote_zh: 输掉冠军赛后，全队垂头丧气地离开了球场。
ex_en: Dejection is a heavy coat; take it off when the sun of hope rises.
ex_zh: 沮丧是一件沉重的外套；当希望的太阳升起时，脱掉它。

def_en: Reliable; able to be trusted
quote_en: My older brother is dependable; he always picks me up from school on time.
quote_zh: 我哥哥很可靠，他总是准时来学校接我。
ex_en: Be so dependable that your word becomes a promise even before you speak it.
ex_zh: 要做到如此可靠，以至于你的话在你还没说出口之前就已经是一个承诺。

def_en: Needing someone or something for support
quote_en: Young birds are dependent on their parents for food and warmth.
quote_zh: 幼鸟依赖父母提供食物和温暖。
ex_en: It is good to be dependent on those who love you; it is wisdom to become less dependent each year.
ex_zh: 依赖爱你的人是好的；每年变得更少依赖则是智慧。

def_en: A prolonged feeling of sadness; a low area of land
quote_en: The patient sought help for her depression and started talking to a therapist.
quote_zh: 病人因抑郁寻求帮助，并开始与治疗师交谈。
ex_en: Depression lies to you; it says you are alone. You are not—reach out a hand.
ex_zh: 抑郁会对你撒谎；它说你是孤独的。你不是——伸出一只手。

def_en: Mocking or scornful
quote_en: The other students made derisive comments about his hand‑me‑down clothes.
quote_zh: 其他学生对他穿旧衣服发出嘲弄的评论。
ex_en: Meet derisive laughter with quiet dignity; a rock does not argue with the wind.
ex_zh: 以安静的尊严面对嘲弄的笑声；岩石不与风争辩。

def_en: To invent or plan carefully
quote_en: The engineers worked together to devise a safer car seat for children.
quote_zh: 工程师们一起设计了一种更安全的儿童汽车座椅。
ex_en: Before you devise a solution, make sure you understand the problem—otherwise you may solve the wrong thing.
ex_zh: 在设计解决方案之前，确保你理解了问题——否则你可能解决了错误的事情。

def_en: Hard-working and careful
quote_en: The diligent student reviewed her notes every night before the exam.
quote_zh: 勤奋的学生在考试前每晚复习笔记。
ex_en: A diligent person moves mountains one stone at a time; the lazy person wonders why the mountain is still there.
ex_zh: 勤奋的人一块石头一块石头地移山；懒惰的人纳闷为什么山还在那里。

def_en: To release or let out; to perform a duty
quote_en: The hospital will discharge the patient when he is healthy enough to go home.
quote_zh: 当病人足够健康可以回家时，医院会让他出院。
ex_en: To discharge a duty without love is to ring a bell that no one hears.
ex_zh: 不带爱去履行职责，就像敲响无人听见的钟。

def_en: To send away; to reject as unimportant
quote_en: The teacher dismissed the class early because of the snowstorm.
quote_zh: 因为暴风雪，老师提前下课了。
ex_en: Do not dismiss a small kindness as meaningless; a single match can light a forest fire of hope.
ex_zh: 不要把小小的善意当作毫无意义；一根火柴就能点燃希望的大火。

def_en: A disagreement or argument
quote_en: The two neighbors had a dispute over where to build the fence.
quote_zh: 两个邻居为在哪里建栅栏发生了争执。
ex_en: In every dispute, at least two versions of truth live; listen for the third—the one neither side sees.
ex_zh: 每一次争执中，至少有两个版本的真相存在；倾听第三个——双方都没有看到的那个。

def_en: To interrupt or throw into disorder
quote_en: A loud noise from the hallway disrupted the math test.
quote_zh: 走廊里的一声巨响扰乱了数学考试。
ex_en: Do not let one bad moment disrupt an entire day; clouds pass, the sun remains.
ex_zh: 别让一个糟糕的时刻扰乱一整天；云会过去，太阳还在。

def_en: Most powerful or influential
quote_en: The dominant player on the basketball team scored 30 points per game.
quote_zh: 篮球队的主力球员每场得30分。
ex_en: The dominant voice is not always right; the quietest person in the room may hold the key.
ex_zh: 主导的声音不一定正确；房间里最安静的人可能握着钥匙。

def_en: A series of rulers from the same family
quote_en: The Ming Dynasty ruled China for nearly 300 years.
quote_zh: 明朝统治中国近三百年。
ex_en: A dynasty crumbles when its people forget why they are a family; so does a home.
ex_zh: 当一个王朝的人民忘记他们为何是一个家族时，王朝就会崩溃；家庭也是如此。

def_en: Using money or resources carefully without waste
quote_en: Buying in bulk is more economical than buying small packages each week.
quote_zh: 批量购买比每周购买小包装更经济。
ex_en: Being economical with money is smart; being economical with kindness is foolish.
ex_zh: 对金钱节约是聪明的；对善意节约是愚蠢的。

def_en: To force something out; to remove forcefully
quote_en: The DVD player will eject the disc when you press the button.
quote_zh: 按下按钮时，DVD播放机会弹出光盘。
ex_en: Do not eject a friend for a single mistake; your own forgiveness account may need future withdrawals.
ex_zh: 不要因为一个错误就驱逐朋友；你自己的宽恕账户未来可能需要取款。

def_en: To come out from a source
quote_en: A wonderful smell of baking bread emanated from the kitchen.
quote_zh: 烤面包的香味从厨房散发出来。
ex_en: Let kindness emanate from you like warmth from a fire—without announcing itself.
ex_zh: 让善意像火焰的热量一样从你身上散发——无需自我宣告。

def_en: The ruler of an empire
quote_en: The emperor lived in a palace with thousands of servants and guards.
quote_zh: 皇帝住在一座拥有数千名仆人和卫兵的宫殿里。
ex_en: An emperor commands armies; a wise person commands only their own tongue.
ex_zh: 皇帝指挥军队；智慧之人只指挥自己的舌头。

def_en: To meet unexpectedly; a brief meeting
quote_en: On her walk home, she encountered a lost puppy and helped it find its owner.
quote_zh: 在回家的路上，她遇到了一只走失的小狗并帮它找到了主人。
ex_en: Every encounter is a chance to leave someone better than you found them.
ex_zh: 每一次相遇都是一次机会，让你遇见的人比你发现他们时更好。

def_en: To gradually take over someone’s space or rights
quote_en: The neighbor’s fence encroached two feet onto our property.
quote_zh: 邻居的篱笆侵占了我们两英尺的地产。
ex_en: Do not let worry encroach on your peace; it is a weed that grows fast if watered by fear.
ex_zh: 别让忧虑侵占你的平静；它是一株杂草，若被恐惧浇灌就会疯长。

def_en: To make someone liked or loved
quote_en: The puppy’s playful tricks endeared him to the whole family.
quote_zh: 小狗顽皮的把戏使全家人都喜欢上了它。
ex_en: Kindness endears you more than brilliance ever could; a warm heart outlasts a sharp mind.
ex_zh: 善意比才华更能让你受人喜爱；温暖的心比敏锐的头脑更持久。

def_en: Full of energy and enthusiasm
quote_en: The energetic children ran around the playground for an hour without stopping.
quote_zh: 精力充沛的孩子们在操场上不停地跑了一个小时。
ex_en: An energetic body is a gift; an energetic conscience is a choice.
ex_zh: 精力充沛的身体是天赐；精力充沛的良心是选择。

def_en: To completely surround or cover
quote_en: Dark smoke from the fire engulfed the entire building.
quote_zh: 大火的黑烟吞没了整栋建筑。
ex_en: Do not let anger engulf you like a wave; learn to stand beneath it and let it pass.
ex_zh: 别让愤怒像波浪一样吞没你；学会站在它下面，让它过去。

def_en: Attractive or tempting
quote_en: The smell of fresh pizza was so enticing that we bought two slices.
quote_zh: 新鲜披萨的香味如此诱人，我们买了两块。
ex_en: The most enticing path is not always the wisest; sometimes the hard road leads home.
ex_zh: 最诱人的路不总是最明智的；有时艰难的路才通向家。

def_en: A messenger or representative, often diplomatic
quote_en: The president sent a special envoy to negotiate peace between the two countries.
quote_zh: 总统派遣特使去谈判两国之间的和平。
ex_en: Be an envoy of hope in every room you enter; leave despair no place to sit.
ex_zh: 做你进入的每个房间的希望使者；让绝望无处可坐。

def_en: A sudden explosion or outburst
quote_en: The volcanic eruption sent ash and smoke thousands of feet into the air.
quote_zh: 火山爆发将火山灰和烟雾送入了数千英尺的高空。
ex_en: An eruption of anger damages more than the target; the speaker's soul is scorched too.
ex_zh: 愤怒的爆发伤害的不仅仅是目标；说话者的灵魂也被灼烧。

def_en: A reckless or adventurous adventure
quote_en: The boys’ escapade involved sneaking out at night to catch fireflies.
quote_zh: 男孩们的冒险行为包括晚上偷偷溜出去抓萤火虫。
ex_en: A youthful escapade is a memory; a lifelong escapade from responsibility is a tragedy.
ex_zh: 年少时的冒险是记忆；终生的责任逃避是悲剧。

def_en: Respect and admiration; to value highly
quote_en: The teacher held her students in high esteem for their honesty.
quote_zh: 老师因学生们的诚实而对他们十分敬重。
ex_en: Esteem is not demanded; it is earned one small act at a time.
ex_zh: 尊重不是要求来的；而是一次次小小的行为挣来的。

def_en: Clear and obvious
quote_en: It was evident from his red eyes that he had been crying.
quote_zh: 从他红红的眼睛可以明显看出他哭过。
ex_en: The truth is not always evident at first glance; patience is the lantern that reveals it.
ex_zh: 真相并不总是一目了然；耐心是照亮它的灯笼。

def_en: To bring forth a feeling, memory, or image
quote_en: The old photograph evoked memories of summer vacations at the beach.
quote_zh: 旧照片唤起了在海滩度暑假的回忆。
ex_en: A single kind word can evoke a smile that lasts all day; try it now.
ex_zh: 一句善意的话语能唤起持续一整天的微笑；现在就试试。

def_en: A statement that makes something seem larger or worse than it is
quote_en: Saying he waited “a million years” is an exaggeration; he only waited ten minutes.
quote_zh: 说他等了“一百万年”是夸张；他只等了十分钟。
ex_en: Exaggeration may entertain, but understatement often tells the deeper truth.
ex_zh: 夸张或许有趣，但轻描淡写往往说出更深的真相。

def_en: A short passage taken from a longer text
quote_en: The teacher read an excerpt from the novel to spark the students’ interest.
quote_zh: 老师读了小说的一段摘录来激发学生们的兴趣。
ex_en: An excerpt of a life—a single kind act—can tell you more than a biography.
ex_zh: 生命的一个片段——一次善意的行为——比一本传记更能告诉你真相。

def_en: To leave out or keep out
quote_en: The club decided to exclude anyone who had not paid the membership fee.
quote_zh: 俱乐部决定排除任何未缴纳会费的人。
ex_en: To exclude someone because they are different is to build a smaller world for yourself.
ex_zh: 因为别人不同而排斥他们，是在为自己建造一个更小的世界。

def_en: Limited to a select few; not including others
quote_en: The private beach is exclusive to residents of the neighborhood.
quote_zh: 私人海滩只对社区住户开放。
ex_en: An exclusive heart is a lonely heart; throw open the doors.
ex_zh: 排外的心是孤独的心；把门敞开吧。

def_en: A short trip for pleasure
quote_en: The science class took an excursion to the natural history museum.
quote_zh: 科学课去自然历史博物馆进行了一次短途旅行。
ex_en: Every day is an excursion into the unknown; pack curiosity, not fear.
ex_zh: 每一天都是一次进入未知的短途旅行；带上好奇心，而不是恐惧。

def_en: The act of spending money or using resources
quote_en: The family tracked every expenditure to save for a new car.
quote_zh: 一家人记录每一笔开支以便攒钱买新车。
ex_en: The greatest expenditure is not money but time; spend it on what outlasts you.
ex_zh: 最大的支出不是金钱，而是时间；把它花在比你持久的东西上。

def_en: Brave or exciting deeds
quote_en: The book told of the knight’s exploits, including slaying a dragon.
quote_zh: 这本书讲述了骑士的英勇事迹，包括杀死一条龙。
ex_en: True exploits are not always loud; feeding the hungry is as heroic as any battle.
ex_zh: 真正的功绩不总是喧闹的；喂养饥饿者与任何战斗一样英勇。

def_en: Outside the regular school curriculum
quote_en: She joined the chess club as an extracurricular activity after school.
quote_zh: 她参加国际象棋俱乐部作为放学后的课外活动。
ex_en: Extracurricular kindness—done when no one is watching—is the most important lesson.
ex_zh: 课外善意——在没人在看时做的——是最重要的一课。

def_en: To send a criminal back to the country where they committed the crime
quote_en: The government agreed to extradite the fugitive to stand trial in France.
quote_zh: 政府同意引渡逃犯到法国受审。
ex_en: You cannot extradite a guilty conscience; it follows you wherever you go.
ex_zh: 你无法引渡内疚的良心；无论你去哪里，它都跟着你。

def_en: Irrelevant or unnecessary
quote_en: The essay was good, but the teacher asked her to remove extraneous details.
quote_zh: 文章不错，但老师让她删掉多余的细节。
ex_en: Life is too short for extraneous worries; prune them like dead branches.
ex_zh: 生命太短暂，容不下多余的忧虑；像修剪枯枝一样修剪它们。

def_en: Beyond normal senses, like telepathy or clairvoyance
quote_en: Some people claim to have extrasensory perception, such as knowing the future.
quote_zh: 有些人声称拥有超感官知觉，比如预知未来。
ex_en: You don't need extrasensory powers to feel a friend's pain; you just need to care.
ex_zh: 你不需要超感官能力来感受朋友的痛苦；你只需要在乎。

def_en: Spending too much; overly elaborate
quote_en: The wedding was extravagant, with a five‑tier cake and live orchestra.
quote_zh: 婚礼很奢侈，有五层蛋糕和现场管弦乐队。
ex_en: An extravagant gift may be forgotten; an extravagant kindness never is.
ex_zh: 奢侈的礼物可能被遗忘；奢侈的善意永远不会。

def_en: To push or force out
quote_en: The machine extrudes melted plastic into long, thin threads.
quote_zh: 机器将融化的塑料挤压成长而细的丝线。
ex_en: Do not extrude your opinions on others; share them gently, then listen.
ex_zh: 不要把你的观点强加给别人；温柔地分享，然后倾听。

def_en: Great joy or triumph
quote_en: The team’s exultation after winning the championship could be heard across the field.
quote_zh: 球队夺冠后的狂喜声整个球场都能听到。
ex_en: Exultation is sweetest when shared; joy split is joy doubled.
ex_zh: 分享时狂喜最甜；快乐被分开，快乐就加倍。

def_en: One side or aspect of something, especially of a gem
quote_en: Each facet of the diamond sparkled differently when the light hit it.
quote_zh: 钻石的每一个刻面在光线照射下都闪烁着不同的光芒。
ex_en: Every person has many facets; do not judge a soul by the one side you happen to see.
ex_zh: 每个人都有许多方面；不要只凭你碰巧看到的那一面来判断一个灵魂。

def_en: Extreme scarcity of food over a wide area
quote_en: The drought caused a severe famine, and many families struggled to find food.
quote_zh: 干旱导致了严重的饥荒，许多家庭难以找到食物。
ex_en: A famine of kindness is worse than any hunger; be the bread that feeds a lonely heart.
ex_zh: 善意的饥荒比任何饥饿都更糟糕；做那喂养孤独心灵的面包。

def_en: Threadlike material from plants or animals; dietary roughage
quote_en: Eating fruits and vegetables gives you the fiber you need for healthy digestion.
quote_zh: 吃水果和蔬菜能提供健康消化所需的纤维。
ex_en: Moral fiber is not woven in comfort; it is forged in the fire of difficult choices.
ex_zh: 道德纤维不是在舒适中编织的；它是在艰难选择的火焰中锻造的。

def_en: Something that is imagined or made up
quote_en: The monster under the bed was just a figment of the child’s imagination.
quote_zh: 床底下的怪物只是孩子想象力的虚构之物。
ex_en: Fear is often a figment of tomorrow borrowed to ruin today—return it.
ex_zh: 恐惧常常是从明天借来毁掉今天的虚构之物——把它还回去。

def_en: Relating to money or money management
quote_en: She sought financial advice before taking out a loan for college.
quote_zh: 在为上大学申请贷款之前，她寻求了财务建议。
ex_en: Financial wealth can vanish overnight; spiritual wealth grows stronger with every storm.
ex_zh: 财富可能一夜消失；精神财富在每一场风暴中变得更强大。

def_en: Patient self-control; tolerance
quote_en: The teacher showed great forbearance when the students kept interrupting.
quote_zh: 当学生们不断打断时，老师表现出了极大的忍耐。
ex_en: Forbearance is not weakness; it is the strength to hold your fire when you could easily shoot.
ex_zh: 忍耐不是软弱；它是当你可以轻易开枪时，却选择不扣扳机的力量。

def_en: To predict or tell beforehand
quote_en: The old farmer claimed he could foretell the weather by watching the clouds.
quote_zh: 老农夫声称他能通过观察云来预言天气。
ex_en: No one can foretell the future, but everyone can shape it—one small choice at a time.
ex_zh: 没有人能预言未来，但每个人都能塑造它——一次一个微小的选择。

def_en: In a formal or official manner
quote_en: The mayor formally announced the new community center at a press conference.
quote_zh: 市长在新闻发布会上正式宣布了新的社区中心。
ex_en: Speak formally when needed, but speak kindly always—that rule never needs an exception.
ex_zh: 需要时正式地说话，但永远要友善地说话——这条规则从不需要例外。

def_en: In the past; previously
quote_en: The building was formerly a school, but now it is a museum.
quote_zh: 这栋楼以前是一所学校，现在成了博物馆。
ex_en: Do not let who you were formerly chain who you are becoming; the door of change swings both ways.
ex_zh: 不要让过去的你束缚正在成为的你；改变之门是双向的。

def_en: A break or crack; to break
quote_en: The X‑ray showed a small fracture in her wrist from the fall.
quote_zh: X光显示她摔倒导致手腕轻微骨折。
ex_en: A fractured bone heals stronger at the break; a fractured trust takes longer—but it can heal too.
ex_zh: 骨折的地方愈合后会更坚固；破碎的信任需要更长时间——但它也能愈合。

def_en: Easily broken or damaged
quote_en: The fragile glass vase was wrapped in bubble wrap for shipping.
quote_zh: 易碎的玻璃花瓶用气泡膜包裹运输。
ex_en: A fragile ego shatters at every criticism; a humble heart learns from every fall.
ex_zh: 脆弱的自我在每一次批评中破碎；谦逊的心从每一次跌倒中学习。

def_en: A small piece broken off from something
quote_en: Archaeologists found a fragment of an ancient pottery bowl.
quote_zh: 考古学家发现了一个古代陶碗的碎片。
ex_en: From the fragments of a broken dream, you can mosaic a new one—if you keep the pieces.
ex_zh: 从破碎梦想的碎片中，你可以拼贴出一个新的——只要你还留着那些碎片。

def_en: Weak and delicate
quote_en: The frail old man needed a cane to walk across the room.
quote_zh: 那位虚弱的老人需要拐杖才能走过房间。
ex_en: A frail body may still house a lion’s heart; never mistake appearance for spirit.
ex_zh: 虚弱的身体里仍可能住着一颗雄狮般的心；永远不要以外表判断精神。

def_en: Careful with money; thrifty
quote_en: By being frugal, she saved enough money for a trip to Japan.
quote_zh: 通过节俭，她攒够了去日本旅行的钱。
ex_en: Frugal with things, generous with love—that is the arithmetic of a well‑lived life.
ex_zh: 对物品节俭，对爱慷慨——这是美好人生的算术。

def_en: The study of the earth’s physical structure and history
quote_en: Geology class took us to the canyon to study layers of ancient rock.
quote_zh: 地质课带我们去峡谷研究古老的岩层。
ex_en: Geology reads the earth's memory; kindness writes the soul's future.
ex_zh: 地质学阅读地球的记忆；善意书写灵魂的未来。

def_en: A slow-moving mass of ice
quote_en: The glacier carved the valley over thousands of years as it crept downhill.
quote_zh: 冰川在数千年的缓慢下移中雕刻出了山谷。
ex_en: A glacier moves slowly, but it changes mountains; be patient—your steady effort will reshape your world.
ex_zh: 冰川移动缓慢，但它改变山川；耐心点——你持续的努力将重塑你的世界。

def_en: A narrow valley with steep walls; to eat greedily
quote_en: The river cut a deep gorge through the mountains over millions of years.
quote_zh: 河流经过数百万年切穿山脉，形成了一道深邃的峡谷。
ex_en: Do not gorge on praise or gorge on self‑pity; both leave the soul malnourished.
ex_zh: 不要贪婪地吞食赞美或自怜；两者都会让灵魂营养不良。

def_en: To please or satisfy
quote_en: It gratified the coach to see his players shake hands after the game.
quote_zh: 看到队员们在赛后握手，教练感到很欣慰。
ex_en: To gratify every desire is to starve the virtue of self‑control; leave some hungers unfed.
ex_zh: 满足每一个欲望，就是饿死自控的美德；让一些渴望得不到满足。

def_en: The natural home of an animal or plant
quote_en: The panda’s natural habitat is the bamboo forest of southwestern China.
quote_zh: 熊猫的自然栖息地是中国西南部的竹林。
ex_en: Kindness is the habitat where trust builds its nest; protect it fiercely.
ex_zh: 善意是信任筑巢的栖息地；要奋力保护它。

def_en: To pay attention to; careful attention
quote_en: The sailor ignored the warning and did not heed the storm forecast.
quote_zh: 水手忽视了警告，没有留心暴风雨预报。
ex_en: Heed the small voice that says “help” when no one else hears it; that voice is your best self.
ex_zh: 留意那个在没人听到时说“帮帮我”的微弱声音；那个声音是你最好的自己。

def_en: Half of the earth, usually divided into northern and southern
quote_en: When it is summer in the northern hemisphere, it is winter in the southern hemisphere.
quote_zh: 北半球是夏天时，南半球是冬天。
ex_en: One hemisphere cannot call itself superior to the other; neither can one kind of heart.
ex_zh: 一个半球不能自称优于另一个；一种心灵也不能。

def_en: A messenger; to announce something important
quote_en: The arrival of the first robin heralds the coming of spring.
quote_zh: 第一只知更鸟的到来宣告了春天的来临。
ex_en: Let your actions herald your values louder than any speech ever could.
ex_zh: 让你的行动比任何演讲都更响亮地宣告你的价值观。

def_en: A picture or symbol representing a word or sound, as in ancient Egypt
quote_en: The Rosetta Stone helped scholars translate Egyptian hieroglyphics.
quote_zh: 罗塞塔石碑帮助学者翻译了埃及象形文字。
ex_en: Every wrinkle on an old face is a hieroglyphic of a life story—learn to read them.
ex_zh: 老人脸上的每一条皱纹都是生命故事的象形文字——学会阅读它们。

def_en: Visually descriptive language in literature
quote_en: The poet used vivid imagery of oceans and storms to express her emotions.
quote_zh: 诗人使用海洋和风暴的生动意象来表达她的情感。
ex_en: The most powerful imagery is not painted or written; it is the kindness you leave in someone's memory.
ex_zh: 最有力的意象不是画出来或写出来的；而是你留在别人记忆中的善意。

def_en: The force of one thing hitting another; a strong effect
quote_en: The meteor's impact left a crater over a mile wide.
quote_zh: 陨石的撞击留下了一英里多宽的坑。
ex_en: You cannot see the impact of a single kind word, but it echoes longer than any shout.
ex_zh: 你无法看到一句善意话语的影响，但它的回响比任何喊叫都持久。

def_en: To force something on someone; to take unfair advantage
quote_en: The government decided to impose a new tax on sugary drinks.
quote_zh: 政府决定对含糖饮料征收新税。
ex_en: Do not impose your dreams on others; the best gift is to help them find their own.
ex_zh: 不要把你的梦想强加给别人；最好的礼物是帮他们找到自己的梦想。

def_en: To formally begin or introduce; to swear in
quote_en: The mayor will inaugurate the new public library with a ribbon‑cutting ceremony.
quote_zh: 市长将以剪彩仪式为新公共图书馆揭幕。
ex_en: Every morning you inaugurate a new day; make the ceremony one of gratitude.
ex_zh: 每个早晨你都在开创新的一天；让这个仪式充满感恩。

def_en: Diligent and hard-working
quote_en: The industrious beaver built a dam that changed the flow of the stream.
quote_zh: 勤奋的海狸建造了一座改变了溪流方向的水坝。
ex_en: An industrious ant outlasts a lazy lion; steady work wins races that speed alone cannot.
ex_zh: 勤奋的蚂蚁比懒惰的狮子更持久；稳健的工作能赢得速度独自无法赢得的比赛。

def_en: To violate or break a law or right
quote_en: Building a fence on a neighbor’s land would infringe on their property rights.
quote_zh: 在邻居的土地上建栅栏会侵犯他们的财产权。
ex_en: Do not let yesterday's mistakes infringe on today's peace; the past has no jurisdiction here.
ex_zh: 不要让昨天的错误侵犯今天的平静；过去在这里没有管辖权。

def_en: To force a liquid into something using a syringe; to introduce
quote_en: The nurse had to inject the vaccine into the patient's arm.
quote_zh: 护士不得不将疫苗注射到病人的手臂里。
ex_en: Inject a little humor into every difficult conversation; laughter is the best syringe for tension.
ex_zh: 在每一次困难的对话中注入一点幽默；笑声是缓解紧张的最佳注射器。

def_en: A short break between parts of a performance
quote_en: During intermission, we bought popcorn and stretched our legs.
quote_zh: 幕间休息时，我们买了爆米花并伸了伸腿。
ex_en: Life itself has no intermission; keep living even when the stage feels dark.
ex_zh: 人生本身没有幕间休息；即使舞台感觉黑暗，也要继续生活。

def_en: Between planets
quote_en: Scientists are studying interplanetary travel for future missions to Mars.
quote_zh: 科学家正在研究未来前往火星任务的行星间旅行。
ex_en: An interplanetary voyage may one day be possible, but a journey from anger to forgiveness is closer—and braver.
ex_zh: 行星间的旅行或许有一天会成为可能，但从愤怒到宽恕的旅程更近——也更勇敢。

def_en: To cross or meet at a point
quote_en: Main Street and First Avenue intersect at the town square.
quote_zh: 主街和第一大道在镇广场相交。
ex_en: Where kindness and courage intersect, great things are born.
ex_zh: 在善意与勇气相交之处，伟大的事物诞生。

def_en: Far away from others; alone
quote_en: The old lighthouse stood on an isolated island, far from any town.
quote_zh: 古老的灯塔矗立在一个偏僻的岛上，远离任何城镇。
ex_en: An isolated heart grows cold; reach out—even a single thread can weave a blanket of warmth.
ex_zh: 孤立的心会变冷；伸出手——即使一根线也能织出温暖的毯子。

def_en: A narrow strip of land connecting two larger land areas
quote_en: The Isthmus of Panama connects North and South America.
quote_zh: 巴拿马地峡连接了北美洲和南美洲。
ex_en: Be an isthmus between angry shores; let peace cross over you.
ex_zh: 做愤怒两岸之间的地峡；让和平从你身上穿过。

def_en: To throw overboard; to discard
quote_en: The captain ordered the crew to jettison the heavy cargo to keep the ship afloat.
quote_zh: 船长命令船员抛弃沉重的货物以保持船只漂浮。
ex_en: Jettison the weight of grudges; your soul will float higher than any ship.
ex_zh: 抛弃怨恨的重量；你的灵魂会比任何船只飘得更高。

def_en: Fond of joking; humorous
quote_en: His jocular personality made him the favorite uncle at every family gathering.
quote_zh: 他爱开玩笑的性格使他成为每次家庭聚会上最受欢迎的叔叔。
ex_en: A jocular word can defuse a bomb of anger; humor is the world's safest explosive.
ex_zh: 一句幽默的话能拆除愤怒的炸弹；幽默是世界上最安全的爆炸物。

def_en: A fastening made by tying; a tangle
quote_en: She tied a tight knot in the rope to secure the boat to the dock.
quote_zh: 她在绳子上打了一个紧紧的结，把船固定在码头上。
ex_en: Some knots of the heart cannot be untied; they must be cut with forgiveness.
ex_zh: 有些心结无法解开；必须用宽恕剪断它们。

def_en: A rope with a sliding loop, used for roping cattle
quote_en: The cowboy swung his lariat and caught the runaway calf.
quote_zh: 牛仔挥动套索，套住了逃跑的小牛。
ex_en: Do not let regret be a lariat that pulls you backward; cut the rope and ride forward.
ex_zh: 不要让悔恨成为把你往后拉的套索；剪断绳子，向前骑行。

def_en: A small printed sheet of paper, often used for advertising
quote_en: Volunteers handed out leaflets to promote the school’s food drive.
quote_zh: 志愿者们散发传单宣传学校的食品募捐活动。
ex_en: A single leaflet can start a movement; a single kind act can start a legacy.
ex_zh: 一张传单可以启动一场运动；一次善举可以开启一份遗产。

def_en: The side sheltered from the wind
quote_en: The sailors anchored the ship on the leeward side of the island to avoid the storm.
quote_zh: 水手们将船停泊在岛屿的背风面以躲避风暴。
ex_en: Stand on the leeward side of another’s anger; do not let their storm blow you over.
ex_zh: 站在他人愤怒的背风面；不要让他们风暴把你吹倒。

def_en: Very famous and admired; from a legend
quote_en: The legendary hero King Arthur pulled the sword from the stone.
quote_zh: 传奇英雄亚瑟王从石头中拔出了剑。
ex_en: You don't need legendary deeds to be legendary to one grateful heart.
ex_zh: 你不需要传奇的事迹才能在一个感恩的心中成为传奇。

def_en: The power to influence people or get things done
quote_en: His experience gave him leverage in the negotiation for a higher salary.
quote_zh: 他的经验在薪资谈判中给了他影响力。
ex_en: Kindness gives you more leverage than force ever will; doors open for the gentle.
ex_zh: 善意比武力给你更多影响力；门为温柔的人打开。

def_en: Direct descent from an ancestor; family tree
quote_en: The royal family can trace its lineage back over five hundred years.
quote_zh: 王室的血统可以追溯到五百多年前。
ex_en: Your lineage matters less than your character; what you inherit is luck, what you become is choice.
ex_zh: 你的血统不如你的品格重要；你继承的是运气，你成为什么是选择。

def_en: Wood for building; to move clumsily
quote_en: The lumber from the pine trees was used to build the cabin.
quote_zh: 松树砍下的木材用来建造了小木屋。
ex_en: Do not lumber through life carrying what you should have left behind; travel light.
ex_zh: 不要背负着本该留下的东西笨重地走过人生；轻装前行。

def_en: Something that attracts; to tempt
quote_en: The fisherman used a shiny lure to attract the big trout.
quote_zh: 渔民用闪亮的诱饵吸引大鳟鱼。
ex_en: The lure of easy money has ruined more souls than poverty ever has.
ex_zh: 轻松赚钱的诱惑毁掉的灵魂比贫穷更多。

def_en: A short poem expressing personal feelings
quote_en: She wrote a lyric poem about the beauty of autumn leaves.
quote_zh: 她写了一首关于秋叶之美的抒情诗。
ex_en: Your life is a lyric poem; each day is a line—make it sing.
ex_zh: 你的人生是一首抒情诗；每一天都是一行——让它歌唱。

def_en: Temporary and improvised
quote_en: The campers built a makeshift shelter from branches and a tarp.
quote_zh: 露营者们用树枝和防水布搭建了一个临时庇护所。
ex_en: A makeshift plan is better than no plan at all, but a character of steel needs no makeshift excuses.
ex_zh: 临时计划总比没有计划好，但钢铁般的品格不需要临时的借口。

def_en: Failing to cope with normal social situations
quote_en: The counselor helped the maladjusted student learn to make friends.
quote_zh: 辅导员帮助那适应不良的学生学习交朋友。
ex_en: A maladjusted world does not excuse a cruel heart; you can be different without being harmful.
ex_zh: 一个适应不良的世界并不能成为残忍之心的借口；你可以与众不同而不伤害他人。

def_en: A person who is always dissatisfied; discontented
quote_en: The malcontent complained about the food, the weather, and even the flowers.
quote_zh: 那个爱抱怨的人对食物、天气甚至花朵都挑毛病。
ex_en: A malcontent finds poison in every well; a grateful heart finds water in every desert.
ex_zh: 不满者在每一口井里都能找到毒药；感恩的心在每一片沙漠里都能找到水。

def_en: Abnormally shaped or formed
quote_en: The malformed branch grew in a twisted spiral instead of straight.
quote_zh: 那根畸形的树枝没有笔直生长，而是扭曲成螺旋状。
ex_en: A malformed body may house a perfect spirit; never judge the vessel by its shape.
ex_zh: 畸形的身体里可能住着完美的灵魂；永远不要以外形判断容器。

def_en: To fail to work correctly
quote_en: The vending machine malfunctioned and took our money without giving any candy.
quote_zh: 自动售货机出了故障，吞了我们的钱却没给糖果。
ex_en: When your conscience malfunctions, stop everything and fix it—it is the only navigation system you have.
ex_zh: 当你的良知失灵时，停下一切并修理它——那是你唯一的导航系统。

def_en: Poor health caused by lack of proper food
quote_en: The doctor said the child’s weakness was due to malnutrition.
quote_zh: 医生说孩子虚弱是由于营养不良。
ex_en: Malnutrition of the soul comes from starving on junk food praise and never feasting on honest love.
ex_zh: 灵魂的营养不良来自于以垃圾食品般的赞美为生，却从未享用过真诚之爱的盛宴。

def_en: Improper or negligent treatment, especially by a professional
quote_en: The doctor was sued for malpractice after misdiagnosing the patient.
quote_zh: 那名医生因误诊病人而被起诉医疗事故。
ex_en: The worst malpractice is not in medicine but in friendship: failing to care when care was due.
ex_zh: 最严重的渎职不在医学中，而在友谊中：在该关心的时候没有关心。

def_en: A metal band for chaining wrists; to restrain
quote_en: The prisoner wore heavy manacles that clanked as he walked.
quote_zh: 囚犯戴着沉重的手铐，走路时叮当作响。
ex_en: Fear is a manacle you lock on yourself; courage holds the key.
ex_zh: 恐惧是你给自己戴上的手铐；勇气握着钥匙。

def_en: To control or handle something; to cope
quote_en: She learned to manage her time better by using a daily planner.
quote_zh: 她学会用每日计划表更好地管理时间。
ex_en: You cannot manage the wind, but you can manage your sails—that is enough.
ex_zh: 你无法管理风，但可以管理你的船帆——这就够了。

def_en: A cosmetic treatment of the hands and fingernails
quote_en: She went to the salon for a manicure before the wedding.
quote_zh: 婚礼前她去美容院做了美甲。
ex_en: A manicured lawn does not make a kind heart; tend to what grows inside.
ex_zh: 修剪整齐的草坪并不能造就善良的心；照料内心生长的事物吧。

def_en: Done by hand; a handbook
quote_en: The car’s manual explains how to change the oil and check the tires.
quote_zh: 汽车手册解释了如何换油和检查轮胎。
ex_en: Manual labor builds calluses on hands; honest living builds strength in character.
ex_zh: 体力劳动在手上有老茧；诚实的生活在品格上建造力量。

def_en: To make something in large quantities by machine
quote_en: The factory manufactures thousands of bicycles every month.
quote_zh: 工厂每月制造数千辆自行车。
ex_en: Do not manufacture worries about tomorrow; today has enough real work for your hands.
ex_zh: 不要制造对明天的担忧；今天已有足够多的实际工作让你的双手忙碌。

def_en: A unit of computer data equal to about one million bytes
quote_en: This photo is three megabytes, so it may take a moment to download.
quote_zh: 这张照片有三兆字节，所以下载可能需要一点时间。
ex_en: A megabyte of data can store a thousand words; a megabyte of kindness stores a thousand smiles.
ex_zh: 一兆字节的数据能存储一千个单词；一兆字节的善意能存储一千个微笑。

def_en: A large stone used in ancient monuments
quote_en: Stonehenge is made of megaliths that weigh several tons each.
quote_zh: 巨石阵由每块重达数吨的巨石构成。
ex_en: A megalith stands for centuries; a kind act stands forever in the memory of one heart.
ex_zh: 巨石矗立数个世纪；一次善举永远矗立在一颗心的记忆中。

def_en: A cone-shaped device for amplifying the voice
quote_en: The coach used a megaphone to shout instructions across the field.
quote_zh: 教练用扩音器在场地上喊指令。
ex_en: A megaphone amplifies sound, but a gentle whisper amplifies trust.
ex_zh: 扩音器放大声音，但温柔的耳语放大信任。

def_en: The basic unit of length; a device that measures
quote_en: The room is five meters wide and six meters long.
quote_zh: 房间宽五米，长六米。
ex_en: Measure your life not in meters but in moments of genuine kindness.
ex_zh: 衡量你人生的不是米，而是真诚善意的时刻。

def_en: A tiny living thing too small to see without a microscope
quote_en: Bacteria are microorganisms that can be helpful or harmful.
quote_zh: 细菌是可能有益也可能有害的微生物。
ex_en: One microorganism can change an ecosystem; one small act of courage can change a life.
ex_zh: 一个微生物可以改变一个生态系统；一次小小的勇气之举可以改变一个生命。

def_en: An oven that heats food using electromagnetic waves
quote_en: She heated her leftovers in the microwave for two minutes.
quote_zh: 她用微波炉加热剩菜两分钟。
ex_en: Kindness is not a microwave; it takes time to warm a cold heart.
ex_zh: 善意不是微波炉；温暖一颗冰冷的心需要时间。

def_en: To move from one region to another, often seasonally
quote_en: Birds migrate south every winter to find warmer weather.
quote_zh: 鸟类每年冬天向南迁徙以寻找更温暖的天气。
ex_en: Ideas migrate from mind to mind; make sure the ones you carry are worth the journey.
ex_zh: 思想在心灵之间迁徙；确保你携带的那些值得这段旅程。

def_en: A person who hoards wealth and spends as little as possible
quote_en: The old miser lived in a tiny house but had millions hidden under the floorboards.
quote_zh: 老守财奴住在一所小房子里，地板下却藏着数百万。
ex_en: A miser hoards gold but starves his spirit; generosity is the only wealth that grows by giving.
ex_zh: 守财奴囤积黄金却饿死自己的精神；慷慨是唯一在给予中增长的财富。

def_en: A person sent to spread religious faith
quote_en: The missionary traveled to a remote village to teach and build a school.
quote_zh: 传教士前往一个偏远村庄教书并建造学校。
ex_en: Be a missionary of kindness; you don't need a passport to spread what heals.
ex_zh: 做善意的传教士；你不需要护照来传播治愈之物。

def_en: To give someone a reason to act
quote_en: The coach’s speech motivated the team to play their best game ever.
quote_zh: 教练的讲话激励了队员们打出了他们有史以来最好的比赛。
ex_en: Fear may motivate for a moment, but hope motivates for a lifetime.
ex_zh: 恐惧能激励一时，但希望能激励一生。

def_en: A story or account of events
quote_en: The book’s narrative follows a young girl who discovers a hidden world.
quote_zh: 这本书的叙事讲述了一个发现隐藏世界的年轻女孩。
ex_en: You are the author of your own narrative; write a story you would be proud to live.
ex_zh: 你是自己叙事的作者；写一个你会为之骄傲地活出来的故事。

def_en: Unclear, vague, or ill-defined
quote_en: His plans for the future were nebulous; he only knew he wanted to help people.
quote_zh: 他对未来的计划很模糊，只知道自己想帮助别人。
ex_en: A nebulous goal is better than no goal at all; clarity comes with walking.
ex_zh: 模糊的目标总比没有目标好；清晰会随着前行而来。

def_en: Failing to take proper care
quote_en: The negligent driver didn’t check his mirrors before changing lanes.
quote_zh: 那位粗心的司机在变道前没有检查后视镜。
ex_en: Being negligent with a promise is like planting a seed and forgetting to water it.
ex_zh: 对承诺疏忽，就像种下种子却忘记浇水。

def_en: So small or unimportant as to be not worth considering
quote_en: The difference between the two test scores was negligible—only one point.
quote_zh: 两次考试分数的差异微不足道——只有一分。
ex_en: A negligible act of kindness repeated daily becomes a mountain of good.
ex_zh: 每天重复的微不足道的善意之举会变成一座善行的大山。

def_en: To discuss in order to reach an agreement
quote_en: The two countries met to negotiate a peace treaty after the war.
quote_zh: 战后两国会面谈判和平条约。
ex_en: Before you negotiate with others, negotiate with yourself: what are you willing to give?
ex_zh: 在与他人谈判之前，先与自己谈判：你愿意给予什么？

def_en: Quick and light in movement or thinking
quote_en: The nimble cat leaped onto the fence and escaped the dog.
quote_zh: 敏捷的猫跳上篱笆，逃脱了狗。
ex_en: A nimble mind is a treasure, but a nimble heart that changes when wrong is a greater one.
ex_zh: 敏捷的头脑是宝藏，但犯错时能改变的敏捷之心是更大的宝藏。

def_en: High social rank; the quality of being noble in character
quote_en: Nobility in the Middle Ages came with land, titles, and responsibilities.
quote_zh: 中世纪的贵族身份伴随着土地、头衔和责任。
ex_en: True nobility is not inherited; it is earned by how you treat those who cannot help you.
ex_zh: 真正的高尚不是继承来的；它由你如何对待那些无法帮助你的人来赢得。

def_en: A short novel, longer than a short story
quote_en: She wrote a novelette about a lost dog finding its way home across three states.
quote_zh: 她写了一部中短篇小说，关于一只走失的狗跨越三个州找到回家路的故事。
ex_en: Your life is not a novelette; it has no word limit—write every chapter with intention.
ex_zh: 你的人生不是中短篇小说；它没有字数限制——用心书写每一章。

def_en: An expression of disagreement or disapproval
quote_en: The lawyer shouted “Objection!” when the witness gave an opinion instead of a fact.
quote_zh: 当证人发表意见而非事实时，律师喊道：“反对！”
ex_en: An objection without a better idea is just a noise; bring a solution or hold your tongue.
ex_zh: 没有更好想法的反对只是噪音；带来解决方案，否则保持沉默。

def_en: Slow to understand; annoyingly insensitive
quote_en: The obtuse student didn’t get the joke even after everyone explained it twice.
quote_zh: 那个迟钝的学生在大家解释了两遍之后还是没听懂那个笑话。
ex_en: Being obtuse is sometimes a choice; choose instead to be curious.
ex_zh: 迟钝有时是一种选择；不如选择保持好奇。

def_en: The beginning of something, especially something unpleasant
quote_en: At the onset of winter, the family stocked up on firewood and blankets.
quote_zh: 冬天开始时，一家人储备了木柴和毯子。
ex_en: The onset of a problem is the best time to solve it; delay is the root of most crises.
ex_zh: 问题开始时是解决它的最佳时机；拖延是大多数危机的根源。

def_en: A short, light opera with spoken dialogue and songs
quote_en: The school performed a cheerful operetta about a prince and a singing cat.
quote_zh: 学校上演了一部关于王子和一只会唱歌的猫的欢快轻歌剧。
ex_en: Life is not an operetta—you cannot always sing your way out of trouble. But you can try.
ex_zh: 人生不是轻歌剧——你不能总靠唱歌摆脱麻烦。但你可以试试。

def_en: Grown without synthetic chemicals; developing naturally
quote_en: Organic vegetables are grown without pesticides or artificial fertilizers.
quote_zh: 有机蔬菜的种植不使用杀虫剂或人造肥料。
ex_en: An organic friendship grows slowly, without force—and lasts longer than any bought or rushed.
ex_zh: 有机的友谊缓慢生长，无需强迫——比任何买来的或仓促建立的友谊都更持久。

def_en: Very intense or too much to handle
quote_en: The amount of homework felt overwhelming, so she made a list and started with one subject.
quote_zh: 作业量感觉难以承受，所以她列了一个清单，从一个科目开始。
ex_en: An overwhelming task becomes manageable when you take the first small step.
ex_zh: 当你迈出第一小步时，一项压倒性的任务就变得可以掌控了。

def_en: Two lines that never meet; similar or comparable
quote_en: The train tracks run parallel to the highway for several miles.
quote_zh: 火车轨道与高速公路平行延伸了几英里。
ex_en: Your life runs parallel to millions of others; a single kind glance can build a bridge between them.
ex_zh: 你的人生与数百万其他人平行；一个善意的眼神就能在他们之间架起一座桥。

def_en: Very dry, especially from heat or lack of water
quote_en: After hours in the desert sun, the travelers’ throats were parched.
quote_zh: 在沙漠的烈日下数小时后，旅行者们的喉咙干得冒烟。
ex_en: A parched land waits for rain; a parched heart waits for a kind word—be the rain.
ex_zh: 干涸的大地等待雨水；干涸的心等待一句善言——做那雨水。

def_en: An official document for international travel
quote_en: You need a valid passport to fly from the United States to France.
quote_zh: 从美国飞往法国需要有效护照。
ex_en: Kindness is a passport that never expires and needs no visa; it opens every border.
ex_zh: 善意是一本永不过期、无需签证的护照；它能打开每一道国界。

def_en: A poor farmer of low social status
quote_en: In medieval times, peasants worked the land while nobles lived in castles.
quote_zh: 中世纪时，农民耕种土地，而贵族住在城堡里。
ex_en: A peasant with a generous heart is richer than a king who hoards his gold.
ex_zh: 一个慷慨的农民比一个囤积黄金的国王更富有。

def_en: A weight that swings back and forth from a fixed point
quote_en: The pendulum on the grandfather clock swung steadily from side to side.
quote_zh: 落地钟的钟摆稳定地左右摆动。
ex_en: The pendulum of fortune swings for everyone; prepare for both directions.
ex_zh: 命运的钟摆为每个人摆动；为两个方向都做好准备。

def_en: Land almost surrounded by water, connected to a mainland
quote_en: Florida is a peninsula with the Atlantic Ocean on one side and the Gulf on the other.
quote_zh: 佛罗里达是一个半岛，一侧是大西洋，另一侧是墨西哥湾。
ex_en: A peninsula reaches out to the sea but stays connected to the land; so should your dreams reach while your feet stay grounded.
ex_zh: 半岛伸向大海却与陆地相连；你的梦想也应如此伸展，同时双脚稳扎大地。

def_en: A regular payment after retirement
quote_en: After working for forty years, her grandfather receives a monthly pension.
quote_zh: 工作了四十年后，她的祖父每月领取养老金。
ex_en: The best pension for old age is not money but the love you invested in others when you were young.
ex_zh: 晚年最好的养老金不是金钱，而是你年轻时在他人身上投资的爱。

def_en: To become aware of through the senses; to understand
quote_en: Bats perceive their surroundings using echolocation, not just sight.
quote_zh: 蝙蝠使用回声定位而非仅靠视觉来感知周围环境。
ex_en: To perceive another’s pain is the first step; to act on it is the second—do not stop at one.
ex_zh: 察觉他人的痛苦是第一步；为之行动是第二步——不要停在第一步。

def_en: The way something is understood or interpreted
quote_en: Public perception of the mayor changed after he fixed the broken bridges.
quote_zh: 市长修复了破损的桥梁后，公众对他的看法改变了。
ex_en: Your perception is not a mirror of the world; it is a painting. Paint carefully.
ex_zh: 你的感知不是世界的镜子；它是一幅画。用心画。

def_en: At an angle of 90 degrees to a line or surface
quote_en: The flagpole stood perpendicular to the ground, perfectly upright.
quote_zh: 旗杆垂直于地面，完全直立。
ex_en: A perpendicular line meets another without bending; so should your integrity meet every test.
ex_zh: 垂直线与另一条线相交而不弯曲；你的正直也应如此面对每一次考验。

def_en: Belonging to a particular person; private
quote_en: Please do not read my personal diary without asking permission.
quote_zh: 未经允许请不要读我的私人日记。
ex_en: Personal success is sweet, but shared success is sweeter—like honey shared with a friend.
ex_zh: 个人的成功是甜的，但分享的成功更甜——就像与朋友分享的蜂蜜。

def_en: The people who work for an organization
quote_en: All personnel must attend the safety training session next Tuesday.
quote_zh: 所有人员必须参加下周二的安全培训。
ex_en: An organization's greatest asset is not its budget but its personnel's hearts.
ex_zh: 一个组织最大的资产不是预算，而是其人员的心。

def_en: A chemical used to kill insects that harm crops
quote_en: Farmers use pesticide to protect their crops from locusts and beetles.
quote_zh: 农民使用杀虫剂保护庄稼免受蝗虫和甲虫的侵害。
ex_en: Worry is a pesticide for the soul—it kills joy along with fear.
ex_zh: 担忧是灵魂的杀虫剂——它在杀死恐惧的同时也杀死了快乐。

def_en: The study of how living bodies function
quote_en: In physiology class, we learned how the heart pumps blood through the body.
quote_zh: 生理学课上，我们学习了心脏如何将血液泵送到全身。
ex_en: Physiology explains the body's machinery; kindness explains why it's worth keeping running.
ex_zh: 生理学解释身体的机器；善意解释为什么它值得继续运转。

def_en: A precious silver-white metal, more valuable than gold
quote_en: Platinum is often used in jewelry because it is strong and does not tarnish.
quote_zh: 铂金常被用于珠宝，因为它坚固且不会失去光泽。
ex_en: A platinum medal tarnishes if the winner has a heart of stone; a heart of gold outshines all metals.
ex_zh: 如果胜利者有一颗石头心，铂金奖牌也会失去光泽；一颗金子般的心胜过所有金属。

def_en: To think about something carefully
quote_en: She sat by the lake to ponder which college she should choose.
quote_zh: 她坐在湖边沉思该选择哪所大学。
ex_en: Ponder the road not taken, but do not camp there—the journey continues ahead.
ex_zh: 沉思那条未走的路，但不要在那里扎营——旅程在前方继续。

def_en: A person who carries luggage, especially at a train station or hotel
quote_en: The hotel porter carried our suitcases up to the third floor.
quote_zh: 酒店的行李员把我们的行李箱搬到了三楼。
ex_en: Every porter carries weight; the heaviest loads are the secrets we never share.
ex_zh: 每个搬运工都负重；最重的负担是我们从未分享的秘密。

def_en: To depict or represent in words or pictures
quote_en: The movie portrays the hero as a brave but lonely figure.
quote_zh: 这部电影将英雄描绘成一个勇敢但孤独的人物。
ex_en: You cannot portray yourself as something you are not; the mask always slips.
ex_zh: 你无法把自己描绘成你不是的样子；面具总会滑落。

def_en: The state of being extremely poor
quote_en: The charity works to relieve poverty by providing food and housing.
quote_zh: 该慈善机构通过提供食物和住房来缓解贫困。
ex_en: Poverty of money is hard; poverty of hope is harder. Be rich in both.
ex_zh: 金钱的贫困是艰难的；希望的贫困更难。两者都要富有。

def_en: Useful and sensible; concerned with actual doing
quote_en: His advice was practical: bring an umbrella because it might rain.
quote_zh: 他的建议很实用：带把伞，因为可能会下雨。
ex_en: A practical mind builds houses; a compassionate heart makes them homes.
ex_zh: 务实的心建造房屋；同情的心使它们成为家。

def_en: A large, flat grassland with few trees
quote_en: The prairie stretched as far as the eye could see, covered in tall grass and wildflowers.
quote_zh: 大草原一望无际，覆盖着高高的野草和野花。
ex_en: On the prairie of life, you are both the seed and the rain—grow where you are planted.
ex_zh: 在人生的草原上，你既是种子又是雨水——在你被栽种的地方生长。

def_en: Not secure; likely to fall or collapse
quote_en: The old ladder was precarious, wobbling with every step he climbed.
quote_zh: 旧梯子很不稳，他每爬一步都在摇晃。
ex_en: A precarious peace is better than a stable war; hold it gently but firmly.
ex_zh: 不稳定的和平胜过稳定的战争；温柔但坚定地握住它。

def_en: A substance added to food to prevent decay
quote_en: Jams contain preservatives to keep them from spoiling quickly.
quote_zh: 果酱含有防腐剂以防止快速变质。
ex_en: The best preservative for a friendship is not silence but honest forgiveness.
ex_zh: 友谊最好的防腐剂不是沉默，而是诚实的宽恕。

def_en: To suppose something is true without proof; to dare without permission
quote_en: I presume you have finished your homework, since you are watching TV.
quote_zh: 我假定你已经完成了作业，因为你正在看电视。
ex_en: Do not presume to know another’s heart; ask, then listen.
ex_zh: 不要擅自揣测他人的心；去问，然后倾听。

def_en: An assumption; behavior that is too bold
quote_en: It is a dangerous presumption to think you know what everyone else is thinking.
quote_zh: 以为你知道其他所有人的想法是一个危险的假定。
ex_en: Presumption closes doors; curiosity opens them. Choose your key.
ex_zh: 假定关上门；好奇心打开门。选择你的钥匙。

def_en: An established or official way of doing something
quote_en: The safety procedure for a fire drill is to leave the building calmly.
quote_zh: 消防演习的安全程序是平静地离开大楼。
ex_en: A procedure without compassion is a machine; add heart, and it becomes a ritual of care.
ex_zh: 没有同情的程序是机器；加上心，它就变成了关怀的仪式。

def_en: To poke or push gently; to encourage someone to act
quote_en: She prodded her sleepy brother with a spoon to wake him for breakfast.
quote_zh: 她用勺子戳了戳困倦的弟弟，叫醒他吃早餐。
ex_en: Sometimes life must prod you forward; better to walk than to wait for the poke.
ex_zh: 有时生活必须推你前进；主动行走比等着被戳更好。

def_en: Happening step by step; favoring new ideas
quote_en: The school has a progressive curriculum that focuses on creativity.
quote_zh: 这所学校有一套注重创造力的进步课程。
ex_en: A progressive society is not one without problems, but one that learns from yesterday's mistakes.
ex_zh: 进步的社会不是没有问题，而是从昨天的错误中学习的社会。

def_en: An object thrown or fired through the air
quote_en: The catapult launched a flaming projectile over the castle wall.
quote_zh: 投石机将一个燃烧的射弹发射过城堡的城墙。
ex_en: Words can be projectiles; aim them to heal, not to wound.
ex_zh: 言语可以是抛射物；瞄准它们去治愈，而非伤害。

def_en: An estimate of future possibilities; a protruding part
quote_en: The weather projection calls for rain all weekend, so bring your boots.
quote_zh: 天气预报预测整个周末有雨，所以带上你的靴子。
ex_en: The worst projection is not of the weather, but of your fears onto tomorrow.
ex_zh: 最糟糕的预测不是天气，而是把你的恐惧投射到明天。

def_en: A suggested plan or offer
quote_en: The businessman made a proposition to buy the old factory and turn it into apartments.
quote_zh: 商人提议购买旧工厂并将其改建成公寓。
ex_en: A bold proposition without kindness is just noise; add heart, and it becomes a movement.
ex_zh: 没有善意的大胆主张只是噪音；加上心，它就变成了一场运动。

def_en: The state of being successful and wealthy
quote_en: The town enjoyed years of prosperity after the new factory opened.
quote_zh: 新工厂开工后，小镇享受了多年的繁荣。
ex_en: Prosperity shared is prosperity multiplied; hoarded, it turns to rust.
ex_zh: 分享的繁荣会倍增；囤积的繁荣会生锈。

def_en: A nutrient essential for building and repairing body tissues
quote_en: Eggs, beans, and chicken are all good sources of protein.
quote_zh: 鸡蛋、豆类和鸡肉都是蛋白质的良好来源。
ex_en: Protein builds muscles; gratitude builds a resilient soul—feed both daily.
ex_zh: 蛋白质建造肌肉；感恩建造坚韧的灵魂——每天滋养两者。

def_en: To cause a reaction, often anger
quote_en: The bully tried to provoke the smaller boy by calling him names.
quote_zh: 霸凌者试图通过辱骂来挑衅那个较小的男孩。
ex_en: Do not let a stranger's word provoke your peace; you are the gatekeeper of your calm.
ex_zh: 不要让陌生人的话语激怒你的平静；你是自己平静的守门人。

def_en: The scientific study of the mind and behavior
quote_en: In psychology class, she learned why people dream and what memories are stored.
quote_zh: 在心理学课上，她学到了人为什么会做梦以及记忆是如何储存的。
ex_en: Psychology explains why we fall; wisdom teaches us how to rise.
ex_zh: 心理学解释我们为何跌倒；智慧教导我们如何站起。

def_en: Extreme or very different from the usual
quote_en: The new principal made radical changes to the school schedule.
quote_zh: 新校长对学校作息时间表做了根本性的改变。
ex_en: A radical idea is not always wrong; yesterday's radical is today's normal.
ex_zh: 激进的想法不总是错的；昨天的激进就是今天的常态。

def_en: Done without method or conscious choice
quote_en: The teacher used a random name generator to pick who would answer first.
quote_zh: 老师用随机名字生成器来挑选谁先回答。
ex_en: A random act of kindness can change a life—no appointment needed.
ex_zh: 一次随机的善举可以改变一个生命——无需预约。

def_en: Acting without careful thought
quote_en: It was rash to quit his job before finding a new one.
quote_zh: 在找到新工作之前辞职是鲁莽的。
ex_en: A rash word flies like an arrow; you cannot call it back. Think before you shoot.
ex_zh: 一句轻率的话像箭一样飞出；你无法叫它回来。射出之前想一想。

def_en: A practical way of seeing things as they really are
quote_en: His realism helped the team avoid impossible promises and focus on achievable goals.
quote_zh: 他的务实帮助团队避免了不可能的承诺，专注于可实现的目标。
ex_en: Realism without hope is cynicism; hope without realism is fantasy. Balance both.
ex_zh: 没有希望的现实主义是玩世不恭；没有现实主义的希望是幻想。平衡两者。

def_en: The back part; to bring up children or animals
quote_en: The car’s rear window was covered in snow after the storm.
quote_zh: 暴风雪后，汽车的后窗被雪覆盖。
ex_en: Do not look to the rear with regret; the road ahead still needs your footsteps.
ex_zh: 不要带着遗憾向后看；前方的路仍然需要你的脚步。

def_en: A partial refund after a purchase
quote_en: The store offered a $20 rebate if you bought the printer and ink together.
quote_zh: 如果你一起购买打印机和墨水，商店提供20美元的返利。
ex_en: Life gives no rebate for wasted days; spend each one with intention.
ex_zh: 人生不为虚度的日子提供返利；用心度过每一天。

def_en: A refutation or counterargument
quote_en: The scientist prepared a strong rebuttal to the critic's claims.
quote_zh: 科学家准备了有力的反驳来回应批评者的主张。
ex_en: A rebuttal without listening is just an argument waiting to happen.
ex_zh: 不听的反驳只是一场等待发生的争论。

def_en: Compensation for loss or injury
quote_en: The company paid recompense to the workers who were injured on the job.
quote_zh: 公司向在工作中受伤的工人支付了赔偿。
ex_en: The best recompense for a kindness is not a reward, but another kindness.
ex_zh: 对善意的最好回报不是奖赏，而是另一份善意。

def_en: To make up for something bad; to exchange for something better
quote_en: He tried to redeem his mistake by apologizing and fixing the problem.
quote_zh: 他试图通过道歉并解决问题来挽回自己的错误。
ex_en: You cannot redeem yesterday, but you can redeem this moment—and that changes tomorrow.
ex_zh: 你无法挽回昨天，但你可以挽回这一刻——而这一刻会改变明天。

def_en: The act of being saved from error or evil
quote_en: The story of the thief’s redemption moved everyone in the courtroom to tears.
quote_zh: 那个小偷的救赎故事让法庭里的每个人都感动落泪。
ex_en: Redemption is not a single leap but a thousand small steps in the right direction.
ex_zh: 救赎不是一次跳跃，而是朝着正确方向的千百个小步。

def_en: No longer needed or useful; unnecessary repetition
quote_en: With the new software, many of the old data entries became redundant.
quote_zh: 有了新软件，许多旧的数据条目变得多余了。
ex_en: A redundant word weakens a sentence; a redundant worry weakens a soul.
ex_zh: 多余的词语削弱句子；多余的担忧削弱灵魂。

def_en: To stop oneself from doing something; a repeated line in a song
quote_en: Please refrain from talking during the movie’s most important scene.
quote_zh: 请在电影最重要的场景期间克制不要说话。
ex_en: Refrain from judging until you have walked in their shoes—the view is different there.
ex_zh: 在穿上他们的鞋子行走之前，克制评判——那里的风景不同。

def_en: To repay someone for an expense
quote_en: The company will reimburse you for any travel costs you pay out of pocket.
quote_zh: 公司将报销你自付的任何差旅费用。
ex_en: Time lost cannot be reimbursed; spend it where your heart truly lives.
ex_zh: 失去的时间无法偿还；把它花在你心真正所在之处。

def_en: To refuse to accept or consider
